title: Playground v2.5 OpenVINO-INT8 — CPU text2image latency (dispatch asset-gen, UC-010 Phase 2b)
grade: proof / single-run (NOT a rigorous multi-run benchmark)

hardware.gpu_present: Intel Arc 140V (Xe2) — NOT used for this run (GPU occupied by the dispatch 30B coder in OVMS)

software.venv: isolated build venv (blarai-build/img-convert/venv311), NOT the BlarAI runtime .venv

methodology.note: latency is wall-clock for pipe(...) only; model load reported separately

not_measured: GPU latency (the GPU was occupied by the dispatch 30B; the GPU asset path is the Phase-3 swap sequence)
co-resident memory cost (this is a standalone CPU run)
1024x1024 latency (the model's native resolution; not run on CPU for time)
finding: CPU generation is usable for a standalone proof but slow (~8 min at 768x768/28 steps). The gen->rembg->cutout pipeline is proven; rembg removes the cluttered background in <1s when the generation yields a distinct subject. Generation prompt/setting tuning is the quality lever (over-emphasising empty space pales the subject; under-constraining lets the mosaic clutter fill the frame) and iterates much faster on the GPU (the Phase-3 path).
